description_long: This bungalow features a classic pitched red-tiled roof with multiple gables and a prominent chimney. The exterior walls are a combination of white and black, with black timber framing details that evoke a Tudor Revival style. There is a spacious, fenced patio area on one side, and a large tree provides shade near the entrance. Windows are arranged asymmetrically, with a mix of single and grouped panes, and a small porch is visible at the front. The property is bordered by a low wall and includes a paved driveway or walkway leading to the entrance. The overall design is compact yet detailed, with clear residential and suburban characteristics.
description: A Tudor Revival-style bungalow with a red-tiled gable roof, black-and-white timber exterior, fenced patio, and asymmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, red-tiled roof, and black-and-white timber facade with a fenced patio to the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long gable roof, a large tree near the entrance, and the white boundary wall extending along the property.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back gable, dense foliage, and the paved area leading to the back entrance.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the patio area, exterior staircase, and the continuation of the black-and-white timber design.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped and single windows
